hero.name: Malothu Likhith
hero.title: Electrical Engineer  |  ML & AI Enthusiast
hero.tagline: Building intelligent systems at the intersection of Machine Learning and Electrical Engineering.

about.description: I'm a Pre final-year Electrical Engineering student with Minor in Computer Science, with a deep passion for Machine Learning and Artificial Intelligence. I love bridging the gap between hardware and intelligent software — from designing control systems to building ML pipelines that solve real-world problems. I've worked on projects spanning computer vision, signal processing, and predictive modelling, and I'm always looking for opportunities to apply engineering principles to data-driven challenges.
highlights: 🎓 B.Tech in Electrical Engineering & Minor in Computer Science
🤖 Passionate about ML, Computer Vision & AI

projects.title: Face Morphing Attack Detection
projects.description: Developed an ML pipeline to detect face morphing attacks in biometric systems using a combination of Local Binary Patterns (LBP) and Discrete Cosine Transform (DCT) features fed into an SVM classifier. Achieved high accuracy on standard morphing attack datasets.

projects.title: Power System Fault Detection
projects.description: Built a machine learning model to classify and detect faults in electrical power transmission lines using current and voltage signal features. Used Random Forest and SVM classifiers with MATLAB-generated datasets.

projects.title: Object Detection & Tracking System
projects.description: Implemented a real-time object detection and tracking system using OpenCV and a pre-trained YOLO model. The system can track multiple objects across video frames with bounding box annotations and confidence scores.

projects.title: PID Controller Simulation
projects.description: Designed and simulated a PID controller for a DC motor speed control system in MATLAB/Simulink. Analysed system response, tuned controller parameters, and compared performance across P, PI, and PID configurations.

experience.description: Worked on building and evaluating supervised ML models for classification tasks. Preprocessed datasets, performed feature engineering, and visualised model performance metrics using Python libraries.
Leading development of a biometric security system that detects morphing attacks on passport-style face images. Implemented feature extraction pipelines and benchmarked multiple classifiers.

experience.company: IEEE — Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers
experience.description: Active member of the IEEE student chapter. Participated in technical workshops, paper presentations, and collaborative projects focused on emerging technologies in electrical and computer engineering.
